label: Report shape — three layers (machine JSON + human markdown + dashboard card) mirroring Lane B's report.json pattern with an added human-readable narrative. JSON is primary artifact; markdown derives from same data; dashboard provides at-a-glance monitoring.

Machine JSON schema (report.json) — 5 top-level sections: metadata (skill slug/path, executor, scoringMethod), dimensions (D1a/D1b/D2/D3/D4/D5 with raw/normalized/weighted/variance fields), composite (overallScore, utilizationRate, utilizationMatrix, variance), bottlenecks (ranked Remediation[]), and scenarios (full per-scenario detail + skillOffBaseline).

Scoring rollup — three-stage aggregation: Stage 1 per-scenario sub-scores (D1a binary, D1b post-hoc set-membership, D2 hitRate × (1-fpPenalty), D3 inverse tool-call count, D4 LLM-as-judge delta, D5 static connectivity). Stage 2 per-dimension mean + variance across scenarios. Stage 3 weighted-sum composite (0-100). All intermediate scores preserved for audit.

Confirmed dimension weights — D1a=0.15, D1b=0.15, D2=0.20, D3=0.15, D4=0.25, D5=0.10. Grounded in Lane A's existing 5-dimension weights and iteration-001 recommendations. D1 routing = 0.30 total, D4 usefulness = highest single weight.

Remediation taxonomy — 10 categories (MISSING_TRIGGER, AMBIGUOUS_TRIGGER, DEAD_INTENT_KEY, ORPHANED_RESOURCE, BROKEN_PATH, OVER_LOADING, UNDER_LOADING, CONFUSION_NEAR_NEIGHBOR, TOO_BROAD_INTENT, MISSING_NEGATIVE_GUIDANCE). Each maps to a concrete file-level change. Each entry carries estimatedImpact, targetFile, action, changeDescription, difficulty, and verification for direct Lane A consumption.

Bottleneck ranking algorithm — estimatedImpact = (1 - dimScore.normalized) × dimWeight × affectedScenarioCount. Favors high-weight dimensions with widespread impact. Severity thresholds: CRITICAL = D1a<20, HIGH = score<50 or >50%affected, MEDIUM = 50-69, LOW = 70-89. Scores>=90 generate no findings.

Human markdown report skeleton — 10-section structure modeled after sk-doc benchmark report template + Llama 3 evaluation methodology. Sections: Executive Summary, Dimension Radar, Utilization Matrix, Routing Health, Resource Discovery, Efficiency, Usefulness, Structural Health, Ranked Bottlenecks, Methodology Notes. Pass/Fail thresholds: PASS >=80 no CRITICAL, NEEDS_IMPROVEMENT 50-79 or HIGH, CRITICAL <50.

Dashboard integration — reducer gains optional skillBenchmarkReports key in experiment-registry.json with per-skill composite scores, statuses, top bottlenecks, utilization rate, and scenario summary. Follows existing graceful-degradation pattern (missing → null).

Prior art — Dynabench (Kiela et al. 2021) principle that benchmarks must produce actionable insights, not just aggregate accuracy, directly informs Lane C's remediation-first report design. Llama 3 evaluation methodology provides structural template for multi-category drill-down with failure taxonomy + concrete recommendations.

Lane C → Lane A hand-off contract — each remediation entry is structured as a Lane A candidate (targetFile + action + changeDescription + verification), enabling pipeline: diagnose → improve → verify. Remediation entries carry difficulty rating (TRIVIAL/EASY/MODERATE/HARD) for triage.

Negative scenario scoring — D1a scores 100 when advisor correctly does NOT recommend the target skill (precision measure). Negative scenarios excluded from D1b/D2/D3/D4 to avoid false-penalizing skills for correctly not loading resources when they should not be invoked.
